frameworks.name: fear conditioning
frameworks.one_line: associative learning pairing neutral cues with aversive shock to drive conditioned fear

frameworks.name: semantic generalisation
frameworks.one_line: conditioned fear spreads via meaning-based representations across translation equivalents

frameworks.name: higher-order cognitive theories of emotion
frameworks.one_line: emotion shaped by semantic and conceptual processes beyond perceptual features

frameworks.name: perceptual feature generalisation
frameworks.one_line: spread of fear driven by shared sensory or surface attributes of cues

core_question: Does fear conditioning to a word generalise across languages in bilinguals via shared semantic representation independent of perceptual features?
key_assumptions: translation equivalents share semantic but not perceptual content
self-reported fear and electrodermal activity index conditioned fear
bilingual lexical-semantic mappings allow cross-language transfer
manipulations: which language carries the shock-reinforced word across two sessions
reinforced vs non-reinforced translation equivalent within each session
language of the conditioned stimulus (English vs Spanish)
key_findings: fear conditioning transferred to the non-reinforced translation equivalent
transfer was replicable across both sessions in the sample
transfer occurred regardless of which language was reinforced
transfer observed in both self-reported fear and electrodermal activity
interpretations: emotional learning modifies semantic word representations beyond perceptual features
semantic similarity supports cross-language generalisation of conditioned fear
